feature_summary: alias-aware TRAIN-only candidate generation over preferred terms and synonyms
character plus word TF-IDF terminology retrieval fused with historical expert-coding memory
auditable matched-alias and score-component provenance for each candidate
fixed-candidate DeepSeek V4-Pro reranking with one grounded rationale per displayed option
disjoint target-domain policy calibration with conservative one-sided accuracy lower-bound selection
persistent review, feedback, and replayable audit infrastructure inherited from v0.1

evaluation_boundary.candidate_space: TRAIN-derived MedDRA code aliases; closed-code diagnostic

deepseek_end_to_end_real_result.claim_boundary: The fixed-seed 50-case paired subset is supportive evidence only; it is too small for a definitive superiority claim.

selective_policy_confirmation.selection_rule: Maximum policy-calibration coverage subject to a one-sided binomial lower accuracy bound >= 0.95; threshold frozen before confirmatory labels are scored.

superseded_policy_diagnostics: The earlier v0.2 end-to-end diagnostic reused a small non-target validation subset for threshold selection and produced 65.2% AUTO coverage but only 84.0% held-out AUTO Accuracy@1; it must not be used as the release policy.

The v0.2.1 disjoint but cross-source calibration improved methodology, yet its prespecified 95% policy achieved only 91.1% on CADEC TEST; this demonstrated source mismatch and also must not be used as the release policy.
deployment_boundaries: AUTO_CANDIDATE remains a provisional coding route, not a clinical-safety claim.
Public CADEC confirmation does not establish BSRBR performance.
BSRBR transfer requires temporal and participant-level leakage controls, terminology-version audit, and local expert-coded calibration/confirmation.
Restricted or governed clinical text must not be sent to an external LLM without explicit governance approval.
A licensed full-MedDRA open-set evaluation remains separate from the TRAIN-derived closed-code public benchmark.
